# solution.py
"""Team collaboration manager (standard library only, ASCII only).

This module implements :class:`TeamCollaborationManager`, a thread-safe,
in-memory backend for managing users, projects, tasks, messages, performance
metrics and reports for a small collaboration tool.

Design notes
------------
* A single shared :class:`threading.RLock` guards every mutation and read.
  The lock is created once in the shared :class:`_State` object and every
  sub-service stores the very same lock instance as ``self._lock`` so callers
  (and tests) can verify identity with :func:`id`.
* A single global id counter hands out monotonically increasing integer ids
  across every entity type (user, project, task, message, rating). The first
  entity created therefore has ``id == 1``.
* Every public getter returns :func:`copy.deepcopy` results so callers can
  never mutate internal state by holding on to a returned object.
"""

import copy
import csv
import enum
import io
import json
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exceptions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when supplied arguments fail validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation would violate a uniqueness constraint."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enums
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(enum.Enum):
    """Role a user holds within the team."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class TaskStatus(enum.Enum):
    """Lifecycle state of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(enum.Enum):
    """Export format for a report."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(enum.Enum):
    """Kind of report produced by the report service."""

    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data model (mutable dataclasses)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A member of the team."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project that groups tasks and messages."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work belonging to a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message posted to a project or a task, optionally a reply."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating given by one user to another."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sentinel used to distinguish "argument omitted" from "explicitly None".
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Unset:
    """Marker type for the :data:`_UNSET` sentinel."""

    __slots__ = ()

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - cosmetic only
        return "UNSET"


_UNSET = _Unset()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared state
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _State:
    """Container for all shared, lock-protected state.

    Holds the single :class:`threading.RLock`, the time source, the global id
    counter and every backing store/index. Every sub-service references this
    one object so they all share the same lock and data.
    """

    def __init__(self, now):
        """Initialise empty stores guarded by a fresh re-entrant lock."""
        self.lock = threading.RLock()
        self.now = now
        self._next_id = 1
        self.users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self.projects: Dict[int, Project] = {}
        self.tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self.messages: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self.ratings: Dict[int, Rating] = {}
        self.assignee_index: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self.username_index: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.email_index: Dict[str, int] = {}

    def next_id(self) -> int:
        """Return the next globally unique id (thread-safe, starts at 1)."""
        with self.lock:
            value = self._next_id
            self._next_id += 1
            return value


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# User service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserService:
    """User registration, lookup, update and deletion."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State):
        """Bind the service to the shared state and lock."""
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_email(email):
        """Validate an email string, raising :class:`ValidationError`."""
        if not isinstance(email, str):
            raise ValidationError("email must be a string")
        stripped = email.strip()
        if not stripped or "@" not in stripped:
            raise ValidationError("invalid email address")
        return stripped

    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        """Create and store a new user, returning a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
                raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
            username = username.strip()
            email = self._validate_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            if username.lower() in self._state.username_index:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email.lower() in self._state.email_index:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")
            uid = self._state.next_id()
            user = User(
                id=uid,
                username=username,
                email=email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
            )
            self._state.users[uid] = user
            self._state.username_index[username.lower()] = uid
            self._state.email_index[email.lower()] = uid
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id) -> User:
        """Return a deep copy of the user, or raise :class:`NotFoundError`."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._state.users.get(user_id)
            if user is None:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        """Return all users sorted by id as deep copies."""
        with self._lock:
            users = sorted(self._state.users.values(), key=lambda u: u.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(u) for u in users]

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=_UNSET, email=_UNSET, role=_UNSET) -> User:
        """Update selected fields of a user and return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._state.users.get(user_id)
            if user is None:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))

            new_username = user.username
            if username is not _UNSET:
                if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
                    raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
                new_username = username.strip()

            new_email = user.email
            if email is not _UNSET:
                new_email = self._validate_email(email)

            new_role = user.role
            if role is not _UNSET:
                if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                    raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
                new_role = role

            if username is not _UNSET:
                owner = self._state.username_index.get(new_username.lower())
                if owner is not None and owner != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email is not _UNSET:
                owner = self._state.email_index.get(new_email.lower())
                if owner is not None and owner != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            self._state.username_index.pop(user.username.lower(), None)
            self._state.email_index.pop(user.email.lower(), None)
            user.username = new_username
            user.email = new_email
            user.role = new_role
            self._state.username_index[new_username.lower()] = user_id
            self._state.email_index[new_email.lower()] = user_id
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id) -> None:
        """Delete a user if no project ownership or open task blocks it."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._state.users.get(user_id)
            if user is None:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            for project in self._state.projects.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project")
            for task_id in self._state.assignee_index.get(user_id, set()):
                task = self._state.tasks.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has an open task")
            del self._state.users[user_id]
            self._state.username_index.pop(user.username.lower(), None)
            self._state.email_index.pop(user.email.lower(), None)
            self._state.assignee_index.pop(user_id, None)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ProjectService:
    """Project creation, lookup, update and cascading deletion."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State):
        """Bind the service to the shared state and lock."""
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock

    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description="") -> Project:
        """Create and store a new project, returning a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            if owner_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("owner {0} not found".format(owner_id))
            if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("name must be a non-empty string")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not be before start_date")
            pid = self._state.next_id()
            project = Project(
                id=pid,
                name=name.strip(),
                description=description,
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
            )
            self._state.projects[pid] = project
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id) -> Project:
        """Return a deep copy of the project, or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._lock:
            project = self._state.projects.get(project_id)
            if project is None:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        """Return all projects sorted by id as deep copies."""
        with self._lock:
            projects = sorted(self._state.projects.values(), key=lambda p: p.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(p) for p in projects]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=_UNSET,
        start_date=_UNSET,
        end_date=_UNSET,
        description=_UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        """Update selected fields of a project and return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            project = self._state.projects.get(project_id)
            if project is None:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))

            new_name = project.name
            if name is not _UNSET:
                if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                    raise ValidationError("name must be a non-empty string")
                new_name = name.strip()

            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is _UNSET else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is _UNSET else end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not be before start_date")

            new_description = project.description if description is _UNSET else description

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            project.description = new_description
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id) -> None:
        """Delete a project and cascade to its tasks and messages."""
        with self._lock:
            project = self._state.projects.get(project_id)
            if project is None:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))

            task_ids = [
                tid for tid, task in self._state.tasks.items()
                if task.project_id == project_id
            ]
            for tid in task_ids:
                task = self._state.tasks.pop(tid)
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    index = self._state.assignee_index.get(task.assignee_id)
                    if index is not None:
                        index.discard(tid)

            task_id_set = set(task_ids)
            message_ids = [
                mid for mid, message in self._state.messages.items()
                if message.project_id == project_id or message.task_id in task_id_set
            ]
            for mid in message_ids:
                del self._state.messages[mid]

            del self._state.projects[project_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskService:
    """Task creation, assignment, scheduling and status transitions."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State):
        """Bind the service to the shared state and lock."""
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock

    def _add_to_index(self, assignee_id, task_id):
        """Record a task under an assignee in the index."""
        self._state.assignee_index.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task_id)

    def _remove_from_index(self, assignee_id, task_id):
        """Remove a task from an assignee in the index."""
        if assignee_id is None:
            return
        index = self._state.assignee_index.get(assignee_id)
        if index is not None:
            index.discard(task_id)

    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None) -> Task:
        """Create and store a new task, returning a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            project = self._state.projects.get(project_id)
            if project is None:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
            if assignee_id is not None and assignee_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("assignee {0} not found".format(assignee_id))
            if not isinstance(title, str) or not title.strip():
                raise ValidationError("title must be a non-empty string")
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline must not be before project start")
            tid = self._state.next_id()
            task = Task(
                id=tid,
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title.strip(),
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                started_at=None,
                completed_at=None,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
            )
            self._state.tasks[tid] = task
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._add_to_index(assignee_id, tid)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        """Return a deep copy of the task, or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Task]:
        """Return a project's tasks sorted by id as deep copies."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id not in self._state.projects:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
            tasks = [
                t for t in self._state.tasks.values() if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id) -> List[Task]:
        """Return a user's assigned tasks sorted by id as deep copies."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            task_ids = self._state.assignee_index.get(user_id, set())
            tasks = [
                self._state.tasks[tid]
                for tid in task_ids
                if tid in self._state.tasks
            ]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks]

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id) -> Task:
        """Assign a task to a user, moving the index entry as needed."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            if assignee_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("assignee {0} not found".format(assignee_id))
            if task.assignee_id != assignee_id:
                self._remove_from_index(task.assignee_id, task_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            self._add_to_index(assignee_id, task_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id) -> Task:
        """Reassign a task to a different user (alias of assign_task)."""
        return self.assign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        """Remove any assignee from a task and update the index."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            self._remove_from_index(task.assignee_id, task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline) -> Task:
        """Set or clear a task deadline and return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._state.projects.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must not be before project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status) -> Task:
        """Transition a task to a new status, managing timestamps."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            now = self._state.now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            task.status = status
            return copy.deepcopy(task)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Message service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class MessageService:
    """Posting, editing, attachment and deletion of messages."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State):
        """Bind the service to the shared state and lock."""
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock

    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        parent_id=None,
        attachments=None,
    ) -> Message:
        """Post a message to exactly one of a project or task."""
        with self._lock:
            if author_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("author {0} not found".format(author_id))
            if project_id is None and task_id is None:
                raise ValidationError("a project_id or task_id is required")
            if project_id is not None and task_id is not None:
                raise ValidationError("only one of project_id/task_id allowed")
            if project_id is not None and project_id not in self._state.projects:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
            if task_id is not None and task_id not in self._state.tasks:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            if parent_id is not None and parent_id not in self._state.messages:
                raise NotFoundError("parent {0} not found".format(parent_id))

            built_attachments = []
            if attachments:
                now = self._state.now()
                for filename, data in attachments:
                    built_attachments.append(
                        Attachment(
                            filename=filename,
                            size=len(data),
                            uploaded_by=author_id,
                            uploaded_at=now,
                        )
                    )

            has_body = isinstance(body, str) and body.strip() != ""
            if not has_body and not built_attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message requires a body or attachments")

            mid = self._state.next_id()
            message = Message(
                id=mid,
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built_attachments,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self._state.messages[mid] = message
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def edit_message(self, msg_id, editor_id, new_body) -> Message:
        """Edit a message's body; only the author may edit."""
        with self._lock:
            message = self._state.messages.get(msg_id)
            if message is None:
                raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(msg_id))
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author can edit")
            if not isinstance(new_body, str) or not new_body.strip():
                raise ValidationError("body must be a non-empty string")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._state.now()
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def add_attachment(self, msg_id, user_id, filename, data) -> Message:
        """Append an attachment to a message; only the author may add."""
        with self._lock:
            message = self._state.messages.get(msg_id)
            if message is None:
                raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(msg_id))
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author can attach")
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename.strip():
                raise ValidationError("filename must be a non-empty string")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=user_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._state.now(),
                )
            )
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Message]:
        """Return a project's messages sorted by id as deep copies."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id not in self._state.projects:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
            messages = [
                m for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.project_id == project_id
            ]
            messages.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(m) for m in messages]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id) -> List[Message]:
        """Return a task's messages sorted by id as deep copies."""
        with self._lock:
            if task_id not in self._state.tasks:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            messages = [
                m for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.task_id == task_id
            ]
            messages.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(m) for m in messages]

    def delete_message(self, msg_id, user_id) -> None:
        """Delete a message; only the author may delete."""
        with self._lock:
            message = self._state.messages.get(msg_id)
            if message is None:
                raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(msg_id))
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author can delete")
            del self._state.messages[msg_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Performance service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class PerformanceService:
    """Completion metrics, ratings and per-user dashboards."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State):
        """Bind the service to the shared state and lock."""
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock

    def _user_tasks(self, user_id):
        """Return the live task objects currently assigned to a user."""
        task_ids = self._state.assignee_index.get(user_id, set())
        return [
            self._state.tasks[tid]
            for tid in task_ids
            if tid in self._state.tasks
        ]

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id) -> float:
        """Return completed/total for a user's tasks (0.0 if none)."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        """Return mean completion time in seconds (None if no completions)."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            durations = []
            for task in self._user_tasks(user_id):
                if (
                    task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.started_at is not None
                    and task.completed_at is not None
                ):
                    durations.append(
                        (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
                    )
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None) -> Rating:
        """Record a peer rating and return a deep copy."""
        with self._lock:
            if rater_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("rater {0} not found".format(rater_id))
            if ratee_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("ratee {0} not found".format(ratee_id))
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user cannot rate themselves")
            if task_id is not None and task_id not in self._state.tasks:
                raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool) or score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer in 1..5")
            rid = self._state.next_id()
            rating = Rating(
                id=rid,
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback,
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
            )
            self._state.ratings[rid] = rating
            return copy.deepcopy(rating)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id):
        """Return the live ratings received by a user, sorted by id."""
        ratings = [
            r for r in self._state.ratings.values() if r.ratee_id == user_id
        ]
        ratings.sort(key=lambda r: r.id)
        return ratings

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id) -> dict:
        """Return count, average and a deep copy of a user's ratings."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count) if count else 0.0
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": float(average),
                "ratings": [copy.deepcopy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id) -> dict:
        """Return aggregated task and rating metrics for a user."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
            not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
            completion_rate = (completed / total) if total else 0.0
            average_time = self.get_average_completion_time(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
            ) if rating_count else 0.0
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": float(completion_rate),
                "average_completion_time": average_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": float(rating_average),
            }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Report service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ReportService:
    """Report generation and export to CSV or PDF."""

    def __init__(self, state: _State):
        """Bind the service to the shared state and lock."""
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock

    @staticmethod
    def _task_stats(tasks) -> dict:
        """Return common count/rate statistics for a list of tasks."""
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
        not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
        completion_rate = (completed / total) if total else 0.0
        return {
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
            "not_started_tasks": not_started,
            "completion_rate": float(completion_rate),
        }

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id) -> dict:
        """Build a progress report for a single project."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id not in self._state.projects:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
            tasks = [
                t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            total = len(tasks)
            status_counts = {
                TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED.value: 0,
                TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value: 0,
                TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value: 0,
            }
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            completion_rate = (completed / total) if total else 0.0
            now = self._state.now()
            overdue = sum(
                1 for t in tasks
                if t.deadline is not None
                and t.deadline < now
                and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            grouped: Dict[int, list] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    grouped.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)
            per_assignee = {
                uid: self._task_stats(group) for uid, group in grouped.items()
            }
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": float(completion_rate),
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None) -> dict:
        """Build a team performance report, optionally scoped to a project."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None and project_id not in self._state.projects:
                raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
            if project_id is None:
                tasks = list(self._state.tasks.values())
            else:
                tasks = [
                    t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                    if t.project_id == project_id
                ]
            grouped: Dict[int, list] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    grouped.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)
            rows = []
            for uid in sorted(grouped.keys()):
                row = {"user_id": uid}
                row.update(self._task_stats(grouped[uid]))
                rows.append(row)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id) -> dict:
        """Build a performance report for a single user."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self._state.users:
                raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
            task_ids = self._state.assignee_index.get(user_id, set())
            tasks = [
                self._state.tasks[tid]
                for tid in task_ids
                if tid in self._state.tasks
            ]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            report = {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
            }
            report.update(self._task_stats(tasks))
            report["tasks"] = [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks]
            return report

    def export_report(self, report, fmt):
        """Export a report dict to CSV text or PDF bytes."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
                raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
            if not isinstance(report, dict):
                raise ValidationError("report must be a dict")
            if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
                return self._to_csv(report)
            return self._to_pdf(report)

    @staticmethod
    def _csv_value(value):
        """Render a value for a CSV cell, JSON-encoding containers."""
        if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
            return json.dumps(value, default=str, sort_keys=True)
        return value

    def _to_csv(self, report) -> str:
        """Serialise a report dict to CSV text with at least one row."""
        output = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(output)
        rows = report.get("rows")
        if isinstance(rows, list):
            header = []
            for row in rows:
                if isinstance(row, dict):
                    for key in row.keys():
                        if key not in header:
                            header.append(key)
            if not header:
                header = [key for key in report.keys() if key != "rows"]
            if not header:
                header = ["value"]
            writer.writerow(header)
            for row in rows:
                if isinstance(row, dict):
                    writer.writerow([self._csv_value(row.get(key)) for key in header])
            return output.getvalue()

        writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
        for key in report.keys():
            writer.writerow([key, self._csv_value(report[key])])
        return output.getvalue()

    @staticmethod
    def _pdf_escape(text) -> str:
        """Escape a string for inclusion in a PDF text object."""
        text = str(text)
        text = text.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("(", "\\(").replace(")", "\\)")
        text = text.replace("\r", " ").replace("\n", " ")
        return "".join(ch if 32 <= ord(ch) < 127 else "?" for ch in text)

    def _report_lines(self, report) -> List[str]:
        """Render a report dict to a list of printable text lines."""
        lines = []
        for key, value in report.items():
            if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
                rendered = json.dumps(value, default=str, sort_keys=True)
            else:
                rendered = str(value)
            lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, rendered))
        return lines or ["(empty report)"]

    def _to_pdf(self, report) -> bytes:
        """Serialise a report dict to a minimal but valid PDF document."""
        lines = self._report_lines(report)
        content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "72 720 Td", "14 TL"]
        first = True
        for line in lines:
            if not first:
                content_parts.append("T*")
            content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(self._pdf_escape(line)))
            first = False
        content_parts.append("ET")
        content = "\n".join(content_parts)
        content_bytes = content.encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 4 0 R >> >> /Contents 5 0 R >>"
            ),
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
            (
                b"<< /Length " + str(len(content_bytes)).encode("ascii") + b" >>\n"
                b"stream\n" + content_bytes + b"\nendstream"
            ),
        ]

        pdf = bytearray()
        pdf += b"%PDF-1.4\n"
        offsets = []
        for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf))
            pdf += str(number).encode("ascii") + b" 0 obj\n"
            pdf += body
            pdf += b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_offset = len(pdf)
        size = len(objects) + 1
        pdf += b"xref\n"
        pdf += b"0 " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
        pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += ("{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset)).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"trailer\n"
        pdf += b"<< /Size " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b" /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
        pdf += b"startxref\n"
        pdf += str(xref_offset).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
        pdf += b"%%EOF"
        return bytes(pdf)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Thread-safe facade over the user, project, task, message, performance
    and report services.

    All services share a single :class:`threading.RLock` (exposed as
    ``self._lock`` and identical on every sub-service) and a single global id
    counter. Construct with ``now`` set to a zero-argument callable returning a
    :class:`datetime.datetime` to control time (defaults to
    :func:`datetime.datetime.now`).
    """

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None):
        """Create the shared state and wire up every sub-service."""
        if now is None:
            now = datetime.now
        self._state = _State(now)
        self._lock = self._state.lock
        self.users = UserService(self._state)
        self.projects = ProjectService(self._state)
        self.tasks = TaskService(self._state)
        self.messages = MessageService(self._state)
        self.performance = PerformanceService(self._state)
        self.reports = ReportService(self._state)

    # -- Users -------------------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        """Register a new user. See :meth:`UserService.register_user`."""
        return self.users.register_user(username, email, role)

    def get_user(self, user_id) -> User:
        """Return a user by id. See :meth:`UserService.get_user`."""
        return self.users.get_user(user_id)

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        """List all users. See :meth:`UserService.list_users`."""
        return self.users.list_users()

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=_UNSET, email=_UNSET, role=_UNSET) -> User:
        """Update a user. See :meth:`UserService.update_user`."""
        return self.users.update_user(user_id, username=username, email=email, role=role)

    def delete_user(self, user_id) -> None:
        """Delete a user. See :meth:`UserService.delete_user`."""
        return self.users.delete_user(user_id)

    # -- Projects ----------------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description="") -> Project:
        """Create a project. See :meth:`ProjectService.create_project`."""
        return self.projects.create_project(name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description)

    def get_project(self, project_id) -> Project:
        """Return a project by id. See :meth:`ProjectService.get_project`."""
        return self.projects.get_project(project_id)

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        """List all projects. See :meth:`ProjectService.list_projects`."""
        return self.projects.list_projects()

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=_UNSET,
        start_date=_UNSET,
        end_date=_UNSET,
        description=_UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        """Update a project. See :meth:`ProjectService.update_project`."""
        return self.projects.update_project(
            project_id,
            name=name,
            start_date=start_date,
            end_date=end_date,
            description=description,
        )

    def delete_project(self, project_id) -> None:
        """Delete a project and cascade. See :meth:`ProjectService.delete_project`."""
        return self.projects.delete_project(project_id)

    # -- Tasks -------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None) -> Task:
        """Create a task. See :meth:`TaskService.create_task`."""
        return self.tasks.create_task(project_id, title, assignee_id=assignee_id, deadline=deadline)

    def get_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        """Return a task by id. See :meth:`TaskService.get_task`."""
        return self.tasks.get_task(task_id)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Task]:
        """List a project's tasks. See :meth:`TaskService.list_tasks_by_project`."""
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_project(project_id)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id) -> List[Task]:
        """List a user's tasks. See :meth:`TaskService.list_tasks_by_assignee`."""
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_assignee(user_id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id) -> Task:
        """Assign a task. See :meth:`TaskService.assign_task`."""
        return self.tasks.assign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id) -> Task:
        """Reassign a task. See :meth:`TaskService.reassign_task`."""
        return self.tasks.reassign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        """Unassign a task. See :meth:`TaskService.unassign_task`."""
        return self.tasks.unassign_task(task_id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline) -> Task:
        """Set or clear a task deadline. See :meth:`TaskService.set_deadline`."""
        return self.tasks.set_deadline(task_id, deadline)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status) -> Task:
        """Update a task status. See :meth:`TaskService.update_status`."""
        return self.tasks.update_status(task_id, status)

    # -- Messages ----------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        parent_id=None,
        attachments=None,
    ) -> Message:
        """Post a message. See :meth:`MessageService.post_message`."""
        return self.messages.post_message(
            author_id,
            body,
            project_id=project_id,
            task_id=task_id,
            parent_id=parent_id,
            attachments=attachments,
        )

    def edit_message(self, msg_id, editor_id, new_body) -> Message:
        """Edit a message. See :meth:`MessageService.edit_message`."""
        return self.messages.edit_message(msg_id, editor_id, new_body)

    def add_attachment(self, msg_id, user_id, filename, data) -> Message:
        """Add an attachment. See :meth:`MessageService.add_attachment`."""
        return self.messages.add_attachment(msg_id, user_id, filename, data)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Message]:
        """List a project's messages. See :meth:`MessageService.list_messages_by_project`."""
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_project(project_id)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id) -> List[Message]:
        """List a task's messages. See :meth:`MessageService.list_messages_by_task`."""
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_task(task_id)

    def delete_message(self, msg_id, user_id) -> None:
        """Delete a message. See :meth:`MessageService.delete_message`."""
        return self.messages.delete_message(msg_id, user_id)

    # -- Performance -------------------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id) -> float:
        """Return a user's completion rate. See :meth:`PerformanceService.get_completion_rate`."""
        return self.performance.get_completion_rate(user_id)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        """Return a user's mean completion time. See :meth:`PerformanceService.get_average_completion_time`."""
        return self.performance.get_average_completion_time(user_id)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None) -> Rating:
        """Submit a rating. See :meth:`PerformanceService.submit_rating`."""
        return self.performance.submit_rating(rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback=feedback, task_id=task_id)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id) -> dict:
        """Return a user's rating summary. See :meth:`PerformanceService.get_ratings_summary`."""
        return self.performance.get_ratings_summary(user_id)

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id) -> dict:
        """Return a user's dashboard. See :meth:`PerformanceService.get_user_dashboard`."""
        return self.performance.get_user_dashboard(user_id)

    # -- Reports -----------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id) -> dict:
        """Build a project progress report. See :meth:`ReportService.project_progress_report`."""
        return self.reports.project_progress_report(project_id)

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None) -> dict:
        """Build a team performance report. See :meth:`ReportService.team_performance_report`."""
        return self.reports.team_performance_report(project_id)

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id) -> dict:
        """Build an individual performance report. See :meth:`ReportService.individual_performance_report`."""
        return self.reports.individual_performance_report(user_id)

    def export_report(self, report, fmt):
        """Export a report. See :meth:`ReportService.export_report`."""
        return self.reports.export_report(report, fmt)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Embedded smoke test
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _smoke_test() -> None:
    """Exercise the main happy paths and assert a few invariants."""
    fixed = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0)
    clock = {"value": fixed}

    def now():
        return clock["value"]

    mgr = TeamCollaborationManager(now=now)

    # Shared lock identity.
    lock_id = id(mgr._lock)
    for service in (
        mgr.users,
        mgr.projects,
        mgr.tasks,
        mgr.messages,
        mgr.performance,
        mgr.reports,
    ):
        assert id(service._lock) == lock_id, "shared lock identity broken"

    owner = mgr.register_user("owner", "owner@example.com")
    assert owner.id == 1, "first id must be 1"
    worker = mgr.register_user("worker", "worker@example.com", UserRole.MEMBER)

    project = mgr.create_project(
        "Apollo", fixed, datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0), owner.id
    )
    task = mgr.create_task(project.id, "Design", assignee_id=worker.id)
    assert task.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED

    mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    clock["value"] = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0)
    completed = mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    assert completed.completed_at == clock["value"]
    assert mgr.get_completion_rate(worker.id) == 1.0
    assert mgr.get_average_completion_time(worker.id) == 7200.0

    msg = mgr.post_message(
        owner.id, "see file", project_id=project.id,
        attachments=[("a.bin", b"123456")],
    )
    assert msg.attachments[0].size == 6

    rating = mgr.submit_rating(owner.id, worker.id, 5, "great")
    assert rating.score == 5

    report = mgr.project_progress_report(project.id)
    assert report["total_tasks"] == 1
    csv_text = mgr.export_report(mgr.team_performance_report(), ReportFormat.CSV)
    assert len(list(csv.reader(io.StringIO(csv_text)))) >= 1
    pdf = mgr.export_report(report, ReportFormat.PDF)
    assert bytes(pdf).startswith(b"%PDF-")

    # Getter isolation.
    fetched = mgr.get_user(owner.id)
    fetched.username = "hacked"
    assert mgr.get_user(owner.id).username == "owner"

    print("solution.py smoke test passed")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    _smoke_test()
